Committees

Aging and Long Term Care
Earthquake Preparedness and
Natural Disasters
Natural Resources
Policy Research Management
Public Employees, Retirement
and Social Security
Transportation
Select Committee on California
Wine Production and Economy
Ad Hoc Legislative
Tourism Caucus

Assembly
California Legislature

BEV HANSEN
ASSEMBLYWOMAN-EIGHTH DISTRICT

REPUBLICAN CAUCUS VICE CHAIR
RURAL CAUCUS CHAIR

June 19, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Gregory Baugher, Executive Director
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

ATTN: Joe Garcia

Dear Mr. Baugher:

I am requesting written advice on the subject of how to
legally deal with my current living arrangements.

During the time that I am in Sacramento I use an extra room
in the home of a long time friend. I have been utilizing this
living situation for the past two years, usually for two or three
nights a week while the Legislature is in session.

The family that I live with have been very close friends of
mine for over ten years, long before I was elected to the State
Assembly. During the time I have shared their home I have not
paid a direct rent, but instead have paid for incidental expenses
that have occurred, such as: babysitter's car repairs, groceries,
and other related expenses to the couple's young child.

A potential problem arose when one of the couple recently
became a register lobbyist. I am confused on how I should legally
deal with this situation. This living situation is pre existing
to the individual's employment as a lobbyist. I am sharing the
home on a periodic basis, while the couple is present. Lastly, I
have been close personally friends with both of these people for
well over ten years, long before my election.

I would appreciate your advice and recommendations on how we
can best continue this living arrangement and be legally within
FPPC regulations.

Sincerely,,

Bev Hansen
BEV HANSEN

sections.question: I am requesting written advice on the subject of how to legally deal with my current living arrangements. During the time that I am in Sacramento I use an extra room in the home of a long time friend. I have been utilizing this living situation for the past two years, usually for two or three nights a week while the Legislature is in session. The family that I live with have been very close friends of mine for over ten years, long before I was elected to the State Assembly. During the time I have shared their home I have not paid a direct rent, but instead have paid for incidental expenses that have occurred, such as: babysitter's car repairs, groceries, and other related expenses to the couple's young child. A potential problem arose when one of the couple recently became a registered lobbyist. I am confused on how I should legally deal with this situation. This living situation is pre existing to the individual's employment as a lobbyist. I am sharing the home on a periodic basis, while the couple is present. Lastly, I have been close personally friends with both of these people for well over ten years, long before my election. I would appreciate your advice and recommendations on how we can best continue this living arrangement and be legally within FPPC regulations.
sections.question_synthetic: Can Assemblywoman Hansen legally continue her long-standing living arrangement with close friends where she shares a room and pays for incidental expenses, now that one of the friends has become a registered lobbyist?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an elected official to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section is present. Document is dated June 19, 1990 and addressed to the FPPC Executive Director requesting guidance on a potential conflict of interest situation involving housing arrangements with a registered lobbyist.

embedding.summary: Assemblywoman Bev Hansen requests FPPC written advice regarding whether she can legally continue a long-standing living arrangement with close personal friends in Sacramento where she occupies an extra room and pays incidental expenses, given that one of the friends recently became a registered lobbyist.
